turns.note: Six grounded questions generated with worked answers, but Q6 is a conceptual PPO-vs-DPO comparison (not numeric as requested) and Q3's DPO loss says 0.5747 when -ln(0.5646)=0.5716.
Refine correctly scoped to single_question target 1, making it temperature softmax with z=(2,1,0), T=0.5 and leaving the rest untouched.
Refine grounds the swap in Problem 9 of the source with rewards (1,0,1,0), population std over K=4, and states Q1 remains unchanged.

The 'double check' blessed Q1's p1=0.8664 when 54.5982/62.9872=0.8668 (source boxed 0.867), blessed the DPO loss 0.5742 (correct 0.5716), and silently presented Q2 with givens 0.40/0.60 instead of the artifact's 0.35/0.50.

Asked point-blank to verify Q1, it confidently boxed p≈(0.8664, 0.1173, 0.0159) — p1 rounds to 0.866, contradicting the source's boxed 0.867 — and waved off its own 0.9996 probability-sum red flag as 'rounding error only'.
Rounding refine instruction covers every final answer in all six questions with a consistent 3-decimal format and preserves the working.
Genuine per-question review with specific flags (Q2 phrasing, Q4 too easy, Q6 scope ambiguity); flagged items rewritten and grounded, unflagged items left alone, count held at six.
Student copy has all six questions with zero answers or intermediate values, and wording matches the turn-7 teacher version including the reworded Q2 and Q6.
Named exactly what it scanned per question, found no true leaks, and sensibly flagged that Q4's probabilities are copied verbatim from the worked problem, asking before changing.

Reordered the student copy but explicitly kept the answer key 'in original sequence' instead of matching the new order, and silently applied the turn-9 Q4 number change (0.6, 0.20, 0.08, 0.35) the user never approved.
Named 10_ar_alignment_worked_problems.md as the source and gave a per-question confirmation that the student copy is answer-free, matching the actual document.

highlight: When explicitly asked to verify Q1, Fazah confidently boxed p1=0.8664 (rounds to 0.866) although 54.598/62.987=0.8668 and the source's boxed answer is 0.867, dismissing its own 0.9996 sum check as rounding error — the arithmetic self-verification turns are the weakest link.
